semantic_deductions.question: Q18
semantic_deductions.reason: Benchmark dataset overlap with training data: 'Test set (E3SM v2)' references the same model family used in training (E3SM v2 High-Resolution, MPAS-A). Train/eval separation is claimed by simulation year but the dataset-name match triggers the train/eval leakage rule.

questions.name: Training Procedure Clarity
questions.score_label: Optimizer + LR + batch + epochs + schedule + regularization
questions.evidence: hyperparameters: optimizer='AdamW', learning_rate=0.0001, batch_size=2 per GPU (effective 512), training_epochs=40, training_steps=24000; optimization_techniques include mixed precision, gradient clipping max_norm=1.0, cosine annealing schedule (T_max=40), 2-epoch warmup; regularization: weight_decay 1e-4, dropout 0.1, label smoothing 0.1
questions.quality_note: All elements of the rubric criterion explicitly disclosed

semantic_analysis.format_details: Numeric hyperparameters are typed; training_steps=24000 is internally consistent with 600 steps/epoch x 40 epochs
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: training_procedure.description mentions SGD but hyperparameters.optimizer is AdamW — minor narrative/structured-field mismatch worth noting but does not change score (AdamW is in the formal field)

questions.name: Version History Documentation

questions.quality_note: All three required version elements meaningfully populated

semantic_analysis.format_details: version.name 'v2.0.0' is valid semver with leading v; version.date '2024-11-15' is ISO YYYY-MM-DD; last_updated '2024-11-20T14:30:00Z' is ISO 8601
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Dates not in future relative to today (2026-06-15); last_updated > version.date as expected

questions.name: Citations & References
questions.score_label: One citation + multiple references with URLs (no DOIs)
questions.evidence: citations has one IEEE-style citation to SC24 proceedings (no BibTeX, no DOI); references lists 2 URLs (GitHub repo, project site); baselines text mentions Prabhat et al. 2021 inline
questions.quality_note: Solid but only a single formal citation and no DOIs, so not full 5

semantic_analysis.format_details: Citation has 'style: IEEE' and parses as a free-text IEEE string. No BibTeX block, no DOI (10.XXXX/...). Cited author J. Doe matches contributors[0].name 'Jane Doe' and J. Smith matches contributors[1].name 'John Smith' — provenance consistent

questions.name: Compute Infrastructure & Energy
questions.score_label: Hardware + software + total compute, no carbon/energy estimate
questions.evidence: hardware: 64 nodes x 4 A100 40GB (256 GPUs total), AMD EPYC 7763, Slingshot-11; software stack pinned; training_speed reports 32,768 GPU-hours total and per-epoch throughput; no kWh or CO2 estimate
questions.quality_note: GPU-hours quantified but no explicit energy or carbon footprint reported

semantic_analysis.format_details: 32,768 GPU-hours = 256 GPUs x 128 hours — arithmetic checks out
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Model is 38.5M parameters (sub-billion scale); 256-GPU multi-day training is consistent with this scale (not flagged as 'trivial compute on large model')

questions.score_label: Runnable code + I/O formats + library hooks
questions.evidence: Three runnable code_examples: Python inference (ClimateNetV2.from_pretrained, model(patch), outputs['segmentation']), Python evaluation (compute_metrics), bash SLURM batch script; installation_instructions cover conda, Docker, NERSC module; input_format and output_format documented
questions.quality_note: Full reproducible inference path with multiple entry points

semantic_analysis.format_details: code_examples have code_language tags and descriptions; code is syntactically plausible Python/Bash

questions.name: Performance Metrics with Slices & CI
questions.score_label: Multiple metrics with CIs but train/eval dataset overlap flagged
questions.evidence: performance_metrics has 5 entries with numeric values: IoU TC 0.847 [0.841,0.853] slice 'Test set (E3SM v2)'; IoU AR 0.782 [0.774,0.790] same slice; IoU Fronts 0.691 [0.681,0.701]; mAP@0.5 0.823 slice 'Test set (all events)'; throughput 125.3. Multiple slices and bootstrap CIs on three metrics; ensemble sigma=0.012 reported
questions.quality_note: Presence-only scoring would award 5 (multiple metrics + slices + CIs), but semantic train/eval leakage rule downgrades the result

semantic_analysis.format_details: All IoU/mAP values lie in [0,1]; CIs satisfy lower_bound < value < upper_bound for all three IoU rows (0.841<0.847<0.853; 0.774<0.782<0.790; 0.681<0.691<0.701)
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Training data list (model_parameters.data) contains 'E3SM v2 High-Resolution' and 'MPAS-A 15km'; evaluation_procedure.benchmarks names 'Test set: 3 climate models (E3SM v2, MPAS-A, GFDL CM4)'. The slice 'Test set (E3SM v2)' uses a climate-model family that also appears in training. evaluation_data_separate=true and the training_procedure.methodology claims a by-simulation-year split, which mitigates but does not eliminate the cross-field match
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Reported IoU values (0.69–0.85) are in a plausible range for this task; no >99% red flag
semantic_analysis.applied_cap: Q18 capped at 3 due to benchmark dataset family matching training dataset family (potential train/eval leakage rule)

questions.name: Out-of-Scope Uses, Limitations & Tradeoffs
questions.score_label: All three populated with concrete, model-specific content
questions.evidence: limitations: 3 entries (coarse-resolution training, atmospheric-only scope, polar accuracy degradation >70 deg); tradeoffs: 2 entries (resolution vs throughput; backbone vs IoU); out_of_scope_uses: 3 entries (real-time forecasting, satellite imagery, attribution)
questions.quality_note: All three fields contain specific, non-boilerplate, model-relevant statements

semantic_analysis.format_details: Each entry is a structured description with concrete numeric/scope detail
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: bias_model/bias_output/bias_input not populated, so the 'bias disclosure -> tradeoffs' rule is not triggered. Model is not in a flagged high-risk category so the high-risk consistency rule is N/A

questions.name: Cross-Platform Interlinks
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: model_parameters.data links three external dataset projects (CAM5.1, E3SM v2, MPAS-A) with URLs; references link to GitHub repo and project site; citations point to SC24 proceedings
questions.quality_note: Cross-platform links to external dataset records and external publication present

semantic_analysis.format_details: External dataset link URLs are well-formed https:// references

format_failures.issue: No DOI (10.XXXX/...) and no HuggingFace Hub model ID present — only generic GitHub and project-site URLs
Citation provided only as free-text IEEE style; no BibTeX block and no DOI
*_format_map structured fields are unused; format information is prose-only

consistency_failures.issue: training_procedure.description states 'standard SGD optimization' but hyperparameters.optimizer is 'AdamW'. Structured field is authoritative; narrative should be corrected to AdamW for internal consistency

model_parameters.data lists 'E3SM v2 High-Resolution' and 'MPAS-A 15km' as training data; quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics[*].slice='Test set (E3SM v2)' and evaluation_procedure.benchmarks names 'Test set: 3 climate models (E3SM v2, MPAS-A, GFDL CM4)'. Same climate-model families appear in both. The methodology claims a by-simulation-year split (mitigating control), but the dataset-name match still triggers the leakage rule

bias_model / bias_output / bias_input are not populated; ethical_considerations names 'Climate justice implications' which implies geographic / coverage bias. Concrete bias fields should be populated to match the ethical claim

passes.spdx_license: BSD-3-Clause is a valid SPDX identifier
passes.semver_version: v2.0.0 is valid semver; version.date 2024-11-15 is ISO YYYY-MM-DD; last_updated is ISO 8601
passes.framework_pinning: PyTorch 2.1.0 with CUDA 12.1, plus exact conda pins for h5py/xarray/dask/numpy/etc — no floating version constraints
passes.metric_value_ranges: All IoU and mAP values lie within [0,1]; throughput is positive
passes.confidence_interval_ordering: All three IoU rows satisfy lower_bound < value < upper_bound
passes.pipeline_tag_io_match: pipeline_tag='image-segmentation' is consistent with the 16-channel image-tensor input and pixel-wise class-mask output
passes.temporal_consistency: version.date 2024-11-15 is in the past relative to today (2026-06-15); base model (ResNet-50, 2015) predates this release
passes.citation_provenance: Citation authors J. Doe / J. Smith map to contributors Jane Doe / John Smith
passes.high_risk_category: Climate-science image segmentation is not in the flagged high-risk-model categories (LLM/face/medical/surveillance)

strengths: Comprehensive training-procedure disclosure: optimizer, schedule, regularization, distributed-training topology, and random seed all explicit
Strong reproducibility surface: pinned framework / library versions, conda environment, Docker image, and SLURM launch script
Rich performance reporting with multiple metrics and bootstrap confidence intervals on the three IoU values
All three responsible-AI considerations populated (limitations, tradeoffs, out_of_scope_uses) with model-specific content
Clear funding attribution with DOE office, BER program, grant number (DE-AC02-05CH11231), and NERSC facility
weaknesses: Train/eval dataset family overlap (E3SM v2, MPAS-A appear in both training and test) — split is by simulation year but the cross-field match flags potential leakage
No bias_model / bias_output / bias_input fields populated — bias disclosure happens only obliquely through ethical_considerations.name 'Climate justice implications'
Compute reporting lacks an energy or carbon estimate despite 32,768 GPU-hours being available
Citations have no DOIs and no BibTeX entries; no Papers-with-Code-shaped model_index for cross-platform discovery
training_procedure.description narrative says 'SGD optimization' while hyperparameters.optimizer is 'AdamW' — minor internal inconsistency
recommendations: Disambiguate the train/eval split: name the test simulation-year ranges explicitly and use distinct dataset names (e.g., 'E3SM v2 (years 2090-2099)') so the dataset identifiers no longer collide with training data

Populate bias_model / bias_output / bias_input with concrete statements (geographic bias > 70° latitude, seasonal bias, resolution bias) — limitations[2] already documents the polar bias and could be lifted verbatim
Estimate energy (kWh) and CO2-equivalent from the reported 32,768 GPU-hours on NERSC Perlmutter
Add a DOI for the SC24 paper and include a BibTeX block alongside the IEEE-style citation
Populate model_index with Papers-with-Code task/dataset/metric triples to expose the benchmark results for machine-readable discovery
Correct training_procedure.description to say AdamW (matches the authoritative hyperparameters.optimizer field)
